MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Jun-03-2016 Continued To Nov-21-16 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Plaintiffs Depositions And Order Issuing Sanctions
Off Calendar Jul-13-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances.

The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

Defendant The Salvation Army's Motion to compel plaintiffs' depositions and order issuing sanctions is granted in part and denied in part. the request for monetary sanctions is denied. Defendant may orally re-depose Kelly Mitcham for no more than 90 minutes on any pleaded issues that have arisen in this case since his prior deposition. Defendant may propound up to 10 written questions on any pleaded issues that have arisen in this case since her prior deposition to be answered by Dona Mitcham in a verified writing within five days of receipt of those questions per the deposition upon written question procedures. If defendant believes that any follow-up questions are necessary, the parties shall meet and confer and, if they cannot reach agreement, may seek resolution of their disagreement by ex parte application. Defendant has shown good cause to take additional depositions of both plaintiffs, but plaintiffs have shown that the likely harm from a further oral deposition of Dona Mitcham outweighs the need for that deposition. Because plaintiffs' positions on this motion do not lack substantial justification, defendants' request for monetary sanctions is denied. A written order is signed and filed in open court.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Jose Rios Merida; Reporter: Laura Martinez, CSR #11332, Bus. Tel: 415-551-3745. (D302)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar May-16-2016 Text Ruling

MOTION FOR LEAVE TO AMEND 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar May-16-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Francis Shehadeh of Law Office of Francis J. Shehadeh telephone number 415-771-6174 for Plaintiffs Donna Mitcham and Kelly Mitcham.
Steven Werth of Allen Glaessmer Hazelwood Werth telephone number 415-697-2000 for Defendant The Salvation Army.

The matter was argued. After meeting and conferring, Counsel agreed to points one and four of the tentative ruling. In re: PLAINTIFFS DONNA MITCHAM and KELLY MITCHAM'S MOTION FOR LEAVE TO FILE A SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT is granted on the following conditions: 1) plaintiffs must file full non-evasive responses to the following deemed- served interrogatories and document requests within ten days: state all facts that support your claim of neglect of a dependent adult, identify all persons with knowledge of those facts and which facts they have knowledge about, and identify and produce all documents referring to such facts; and 4) if defendant so desires, plaintiff must stipulate to a trial continuance of no more than three months. With these conditions, defendant suffers no prejudice from the granting of the motion and the motion is favored by the strong California policies of allowing amendments to pleadings and deciding cases on their merits. In addition, the parties agreed to take the current motion for summary judgment scheduled for June 10, 2016 off calendar to be refiled to address all of the causes of action (to add the dependent adult abuse claim) on 60 days notice (not 75 days/and will not be within 30-days of the new trial). The parties will also vacate the current trial date of July 25, 2016 and move it to a Monday at 9:30 a.m. in the month of November (parties are to work out a specific date and include it in the order). Counsel will prepare a form of order.

Judge: Harold Kahn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976. (302/HK)
